2606.05463.title: PSEBench: A Controllable and Verifiable Benchmark for Evaluating LLMs in Patient Safety Event Triage
2606.05463.paper_summary: Patient safety event triage, determining whether a clinical event is reportable under jurisdiction-specific policy, is a high-stakes task typically performed manually by patient safety experts. Although LLMs may support this workflow, reliable evaluation is limited by the lack of benchmarks to capture evidence-grounded policy reasoning, proactive information seeking for incomplete reports, and principled abstention in irreducibly ambiguous cases. We address this gap with a policy-grounded construction methodology centered on the clause card, a structured representation that factorizes regulatory text into auditable decision specifications. Combining clause cards with anchor-driven instantiation and closed-loop verification, our scalable pipeline produces narratives with by-construction ground truth and naturally supports generating missing information and uncertain variants. We instantiate this method on Minnesota's 29 Reportable Adverse Health Events, producing PSEBench, a 5,074-case benchmark with an agentic evaluation environment. Evaluation on 15 representative LLMs reveals consistent capability trends, demonstrates the benchmark's utility, and identifies actionable gaps toward reliable LLM-based patient safety event triage.
2606.05463.paper_summary_zh: 病人安全事件的分流，確定臨床事件是否根據特定管轄區的政策可報告，是一項高風險的任務，通常由病人安全專家手動執行。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）可能支持這一工作流程，但由於缺乏基準來捕捉基於證據的政策推理、主動尋求不完整報告的信息，以及在不可簡化的模糊案例中原則性的回避，可靠的評估受到限制。我們通過一種基於政策的構建方法來填補這一空白，該方法以條款卡為中心，條款卡是一種將監管文本分解為可審計決策規範的結構化表示。結合條款卡與基於錨點的實例化和閉環驗證，我們的可擴展管道生成具有構建基準真相的敘述，並自然支持生成缺失的信息和不確定的變體。我們在明尼蘇達州的29個可報告不良健康事件上實現了這一方法，產生了PSEBench，一個包含5,074個案例的基準，並提供了一個自主評估環境。對15個具有代表性的LLMs的評估顯示出一致的能力趨勢，證明了基準的實用性，並識別出朝向可靠的基於LLM的病人安全事件分流的可行性缺口。
2606.05463.author: Keqi Han et.al.
2606.05463.authors: Keqi Han, Ryan Young, Annabel Strauss, Lindsey Hughes, Katharine M. Nesbitt, Nicole Schueler, Che Ngufor, Carl Yang, Yuan Xue, Zhijun Yin
